---
title: "Having trouble installing a startup script"
date: 2006-09-04
forum: General Help
---

### Post by cayblood on 2006-09-04
I just compiled mythtv from source and I'm trying to install a startup script to run mythtvbackend automatically during startup.  My script runs just fine from the command line.

Here are the contents of my script, just in case:

```

#! /bin/sh
#
#  This script was generated by The Ubuntu Linux Startup Script Builder
#  version 1.5 located at http://rob.pectol.com/startup_scriptbuilder/.
#
#  Generated: Mon Sep  4 10:06:34 MDT 2006

PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin

if [ -r /lib/lsb/init-functions ]; then
        . /lib/lsb/init-functions
        logbegin="log_begin_msg"
        logend="log_end_msg"
else
        logbegin="echo -n"
        logend=`printf "echo .\n"`
fi

# Exit if the daemon binary is NOT available, executable, etc.
test -x /usr/local/bin/mythbackend || exit 0

# Start function
d_start() {
        start-stop-daemon --start --quiet --pidfile /var/run/mythbackend.pid --exec /usr/local/bin/mythbackend -- -d -p /var/run/mythbackend.pid -l /var/log/mythbackend.log
}

# Stop function
d_stop() {
        start-stop-daemon --stop --quiet --pidfile /var/run/mythbackend.pid --name mythbackend
}

case "$1" in
        start)
                $logbegin "Starting MythTV backend: mythbackend"
                d_start
                $logend $?
                ;;
        stop)
                $logbegin "Stopping MythTV backend: mythbackend"
                d_stop
                $logend $?
                ;;
        restart)
                $0 stop
                sleep 1
                $0 start
                ;;
        *)
        log_success_msg "Usage: mythbackend {start|stop|restart}"
        exit 1
        ;;
esac
exit 0

```

Anyhow, the script works fine if I call it from the command line, like so:

```

carl@htpc:~$ sudo /etc/init.d/mythbackend start
Password:
 * Starting MythTV backend: mythbackend                                  [ ok ]

```

I set up the script to be run automatically at boot with the following command:

```

carl@htpc:~$ sudo update-rc.d mythbackend defaults 99
 Adding system startup for /etc/init.d/mythbackend ...
   /etc/rc0.d/K99mythbackend -> ../init.d/mythbackend
   /etc/rc1.d/K99mythbackend -> ../init.d/mythbackend
   /etc/rc6.d/K99mythbackend -> ../init.d/mythbackend
   /etc/rc2.d/S99mythbackend -> ../init.d/mythbackend
   /etc/rc3.d/S99mythbackend -> ../init.d/mythbackend
   /etc/rc4.d/S99mythbackend -> ../init.d/mythbackend
   /etc/rc5.d/S99mythbackend -> ../init.d/mythbackend

```

But when I reboot, it doesn't seem to be doing anything.  What am I doing wrong?

Thanks,

Carl
